samples.xml_description: Implement an unsigned 16-bit multiplier using shift and accumulate operations. Clock signal. Active-low reset signal. Chip enable signal to initiate multiplication. Multiplicand input. Multiplier input. Product output. Completion flag indicating the end of multiplication. Shift count register, 0 to 16. Update shift count register. if (!rst_n) i <= 0; else if (start && i < 17) i <= i + 1; else if (!start) i <= 0; Update completion flag. if (!rst_n) done_r <= 0; else if (i == 16) done_r <= 1; else if (i == 17) done_r <= 0; Update product register. if (!rst_n) yout_r <= 0; else if (start) { if (i == 0) { areg <= ain; breg <= bin; } else if (i < 17 && areg[i-1]) { yout_r <= yout_r + (breg << (i-1)); } } The module performs multiplication of two unsigned 16-bit numbers (ain and bin) using shift and accumulate operations. On the positive edge of the clock or the negative edge of reset, the shift count (i) is updated. If reset is active, i is set to 0. If start is high and i is less than 17, i is incremented by 1. If start is low, i is reset to 0. On the positive edge of the clock or the negative edge of reset, the multiplication completion flag (done_r) is updated. If reset is active, done_r is set to 0. If i is equal to 16, done_r is set to 1, indicating the end of multiplication. If i is equal to 17, done_r is reset to 0. On the positive edge of the clock or the negative edge of reset, the multiplicand (areg), multiplier (breg), and product (yout_r) registers are updated. If reset is active, all registers are set to 0. If start is high, the multiplication process begins. If i is 0, the multiplicand and multiplier are stored in areg and breg, respectively. If i is greater than 0 and less than 17, the product is accumulated by adding the shifted multiplier (breg << (i-1)) to yout_r if the corresponding bit in areg (areg[i-1]) is high. The final product is assigned to the output yout.
